# Multiplayer Form with Per-Field Typing Indicators and Field-Lock Overlay

## Background

Liveblocks is a realtime collaboration infrastructure that exposes Presence (ephemeral per-user state) and Storage (CRDT-backed persistent state) through React hooks like `useMyPresence`, `useOthers`, `useStorage`, and `useMutation`. Your project is a Next.js (App Router) app that already renders a static form skeleton (Name / Email / Bio fields) but has no realtime behavior wired up. Your job is to turn it into a multiplayer form: every keystroke is synced across browsers via Liveblocks Storage, and every focused/typing user is visible to others via Liveblocks Presence, with a per-field "X is typing in Bio…" indicator and an optimistic per-field lock overlay.

You MUST use the real Liveblocks cloud — DO NOT mock the Liveblocks SDK, DO NOT stub the WebSocket, and DO NOT swap in a fake provider. The runtime environment will provide a real `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` (server-side) and `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` (client-side, public key starting with `pk_`).

## Requirements

* Implement a Liveblocks-backed multiplayer form using `@liveblocks/client` and `@liveblocks/react` (App Router compatible).
* Storage: a single `LiveObject` named `form` holding three string fields: `name`, `email`, `bio`. Initialize them to empty strings if the room has no storage yet.
* Presence: each user has a `typing` field whose value is one of `"name"`, `"email"`, `"bio"`, or `null`, plus an `info` (or `user`) field with a stable display name (e.g. derived from query-string `?name=Alice`, falling back to `Anonymous`).
* Per-field input: each of the three `<input>`/`<textarea>` elements must be controlled by the value from Storage; every keystroke must write back into the `LiveObject` (e.g. with `useMutation`). The same field MUST stay in sync across all open browser tabs/contexts connected to the same room.
* Per-field typing indicator: above each field, when at least one *other* user has `presence.typing` equal to that field id, render a visible indicator line whose text contains both the user's display name and the human-readable field label (Name / Email / Bio). Examples that are acceptable: `Alice is typing in Bio...`, `Alice is typing in Bio…`. The indicator MUST disappear when that other user blurs / stops typing in the field.
* Optimistic per-field lock overlay: when at least one *other* user is currently `typing` in a field, the corresponding input for the local user MUST show a visually disabled overlay (greyed out / semi-transparent). The input itself MUST remain functionally usable — the local user can still focus it and take over typing (this is a soft lock, not a hard `disabled`). The overlay element MUST be detectable from the DOM (see Acceptance Criteria for the required marker).
* The room id must be the same for every visitor of the page so two browser contexts collaborate in the same room. Hardcode the room id as `harbor-typing-form-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` (read `ZEALT_RUN_ID` from the environment at build/start time; if unset, fall back to `harbor-typing-form-local`). This isolates each evaluation run on the real Liveblocks cloud.
* The Liveblocks auth endpoint MUST be implemented on the server (e.g. `app/api/liveblocks-auth/route.ts`) using `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY`. The client MUST authenticate via this endpoint (i.e. `LiveblocksProvider` with `authEndpoint: "/api/liveblocks-auth"`). Do NOT ship the secret key to the browser.

## Implementation Hints

* The starter project is at `/home/user/myproject`. Inspect the existing form markup in `app/page.tsx` (or equivalent) and replace its static state with Liveblocks-driven state.
* `@liveblocks/client`, `@liveblocks/react`, and `@liveblocks/node` are already installed; don't reinstall.
* Wire `LiveblocksProvider` + `RoomProvider` around the form. Use `initialStorage={{ form: new LiveObject({ name: "", email: "", bio: "" }) }}` and `initialPresence={{ typing: null, info: { name: <displayName> } }}` (field names are flexible as long as Presence/Storage shapes meet the requirements).
* Use `useStorage((root) => root.form)` to read, and `useMutation(({ storage }, field, value) => storage.get("form").set(field, value), [])` to write.
* Use `useUpdateMyPresence()` on `onFocus`/`onBlur`/`onChange` to keep `presence.typing` synced with the field the local user is editing.
* Use `useOthers()` to compute, per field, the list of OTHER users currently typing in that field; render the indicator and the lock overlay based on that list.
* For the overlay, an absolutely-positioned `<div data-testid="lock-overlay-<field>">` on top of the input works well. Pointer-events must NOT permanently disable the input — keep the underlying input reachable by keyboard / click so a user can take over.
* Read the display name from `searchParams.get("name")` on the client; fall back to `"Anonymous"`.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Start command: npm run start (a production build will already exist at verification time; the verifier will rebuild and start as needed)
* Port: 3000
* Route: `GET /` renders a form page with at least three labeled inputs/textarea elements for `Name`, `Email`, and `Bio`.
  * The `Name` input has `data-testid="input-name"` and is a single-line `<input>`.
  * The `Email` input has `data-testid="input-email"` and is a single-line `<input>`.
  * The `Bio` input has `data-testid="input-bio"` and is a `<textarea>` or multi-line input.
* Per-field typing indicator marker: when an OTHER user is typing in a field, an element with `data-testid="typing-indicator-<field>"` is rendered above that field (where `<field>` is one of `name`, `email`, `bio`). Its visible text MUST include both that user's display name and the field's human-readable label (`Name`, `Email`, or `Bio`).
* Per-field lock overlay marker: when an OTHER user is typing in a field, an element with `data-testid="lock-overlay-<field>"` is rendered over that field. When NO other user is typing in that field, this overlay element is NOT in the DOM.
* Storage sync: typing in any field in browser context A is reflected in the value of the corresponding input in browser context B within \~2 seconds, when both contexts are connected to the same room.
* Presence cleanup: when the typing user blurs the field, the indicator and the lock overlay in other contexts MUST disappear within \~2 seconds.
* The Liveblocks auth endpoint is reachable at `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` and returns `200` (or `401` only when the request is unauthenticated in a way the implementer chose) for a normal client request initiated by `LiveblocksProvider`.
* The room id MUST be derived from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable as `harbor-typing-form-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` so concurrent verification runs do not collide.
* The Liveblocks SDK packages (`@liveblocks/client`, `@liveblocks/react`) MUST be used. No mock provider or fake WebSocket may be substituted.
Let me start by exploring the existing project structure to understand what's already there.
Now I have a clear picture of the project. Let me set up the todo list and implement everything.

part.text: Now I need to add `NEXT_PUBLIC_ROOM_ID` to the environment. The room ID needs to be derived from `ZEALT_RUN_ID` at build time. Let me update next.config.js to expose this as an env var, and update the Room component to use the correct room ID format:
Now let me check the `@liveblocks/react` version to understand how to correctly use `createRoomContext` with the suspense API:

I see that `createRoomContext` is exported from the main index, but looking at the version more carefully - this looks like Liveblocks v2 which uses a different API pattern. Let me check the actual API:
Let me look at the `createRoomContext` signature more carefully:

output.content:
type RoomContextBundle

= Resolve & SharedContextBundle["classic"] & { /** * Returns the current storage status for the Room, and triggers * a re-render whenever it changes. Can be used to render a "Saving..." * indicator. * * @deprecated Prefer useSyncStatus() */ useStorageStatus(options?: UseStorageStatusOptions): StorageStatus; /** * Extract arbitrary data from the Liveblocks Storage state, using an * arbitrary selector function. * * The selector function will get re-evaluated any time something changes in * Storage. The value returned by your selector function will also be the * value returned by the hook. * * The `root` value that gets passed to your selector function is * a immutable/readonly version of your Liveblocks storage root. * * The component that uses this hook will automatically re-render if the * returned value changes. * * By default `useStorage()` uses strict `===` to check for equality. Take * extra care when returning a computed object or list, for example when you * return the result of a .map() or .filter() call from the selector. In * those cases, you'll probably want to use a `shallow` comparison check. */ useStorage(selector: (root: ToImmutable) => T, isEqual?: (prev: T | null, curr: T | null) => boolean): T | null; /** * Gets the current user once it is connected to the room. * * @example * const me = useSelf(); * if (me !== null) { * const { x, y } = me.presence.cursor; * } */ useSelf(): User | null; /** * Extract arbitrary data based on the current user. * * The selector function will get re-evaluated any time your presence data * changes. * * The component that uses this hook will automatically re-render if your * selector function returns a different value from its previous run. * * By default `useSelf()` uses strict `===` to check for equality. Take extra * care when returning a computed object or list, for example when you return * the result of a .map() or .filter() call from the selector. In those * cases, you'll probably want to use a `shallow` comparison check. * * Will return `null` while Liveblocks isn't connected to a room yet. * * @example * const cursor = useSelf(me => me.presence.cursor); * if (cursor !== null) { * const { x, y } = cursor; * } * */ useSelf(selector: (me: User) => T, isEqual?: (prev: T, curr: T) => boolean): T | null; /** * Returns the threads within the current room. * * @example * const { threads, error, isLoading } = useThreads(); */ useThreads(options?: UseThreadsOptions): ThreadsAsyncResult; /** * @deprecated Renamed to `useRoomSubscriptionSettings` * * Returns the user's notification settings for the current room * and a function to update them. */ useRoomNotificationSettings(): [ RoomSubscriptionSettingsAsyncResult, (settings: Partial) => void ]; /** * Returns the user's subscription settings for the current room * and a function to update them. * * @example * const [{ settings }, updateSettings] = useRoomSubscriptionSettings(); */ useRoomSubscriptionSettings(): [ RoomSubscriptionSettingsAsyncResult, (settings: Partial) => void ]; /** * Returns a presigned URL for an attachment by its ID. * * @example * const { url, error, isLoading } = useAttachmentUrl("at_xxx"); */ useAttachmentUrl(attachmentId: string): AttachmentUrlAsyncResult; /** * (Private beta) Returns a history of versions of the current room. * * @example * const { versions, error, isLoading } = useHistoryVersions(); */ useHistoryVersions(): HistoryVersionsAsyncResult; /** * (Private beta) Returns the data of a specific version of the current room. * * @example * const { data, error, isLoading } = useHistoryVersionData(version.id); */ useHistoryVersionData(id: string): HistoryVersionDataAsyncResult; suspense: Resolve & SharedContextBundle["suspense"] & { /** * Returns the current storage status for the Room, and triggers * a re-render whenever it changes. Can be used to render a "Saving..." * indicator. * * @deprecated Prefer useSyncStatus() */ useStorageStatus(options?: UseStorageStatusOptions): StorageStatusSuccess; /** * Extract arbitrary data from the Liveblocks Storage state, using an * arbitrary selector function. * * The selector function will get re-evaluated any time something changes in * Storage. The value returned by your selector function will also be the * value returned by the hook. * * The `root` value that gets passed to your selector function is * a immutable/readonly version of your Liveblocks storage root. * * The component that uses this hook will automatically re-render if the * returned value changes. * * By default `useStorage()` uses strict `===` to check for equality. Take * extra care when returning a computed object or list, for example when you * return the result of a .map() or .filter() call from the selector. In * those cases, you'll probably want to use a `shallow` comparison check. */ useStorage(selector: (root: ToImmutable) => T, isEqual?: (prev: T, curr: T) => boolean): T; /** * Gets the current user once it is connected to the room. * * @example * const me = useSelf(); * const { x, y } = me.presence.cursor; */ useSelf(): User; /** * Extract arbitrary data based on the current user. * * The selector function will get re-evaluated any time your presence data * changes. * * The component that uses this hook will automatically re-render if your

part.text: The `createRoomContext` API with `.suspense` is confirmed. Now I need to check the `RoomContextBundleCommon` to verify the `RoomProvider` is there:

The API looks correct. The `createRoomContext` returns an object with a `suspense` property. My `liveblocks.config.ts` correctly destructures `suspense` from the result. 

However, I notice the `RoomProvider` is in `RoomContextBundleCommon` (not only in suspense), so it would be at the top level. Let me fix the liveblocks.config.ts to destructure correctly - `RoomProvider` is common across both, so let me verify my config is right:

Looking at the type definition: `RoomContextBundle` has `RoomProvider` in `RoomContextBundleCommon` which is merged into BOTH the classic bundle AND into the `.suspense` bundle. So accessing `suspense.RoomProvider` should work fine.

Now let me also check how the `OpaqueClient` is created - `createClient` from `@liveblocks/client`:

The current `liveblocks.config.ts` creates the client with `authEndpoint: "/api/liveblocks-auth"` which is correct. Let me verify the Room component properly passes the display name from search params. There's an issue - the presence `info.name` is set to the initial value `"Anonymous"` but should update once the component mounts on the client. Let me fix the Room component to handle this more robustly by updating presence after mount:
Now let's build the project to verify everything compiles:

Create multiplayer form component with Storage, Presence, typing indicators, lock overlays
Update layout.tsx to wrap with providers
Update page.tsx to use the multiplayer form
Build and verify the project compiles correctly

part.text: The error is clear: I'm using Suspense versions of the hooks server-side. I need to use `ClientSideSuspense` from Liveblocks, or restructure to use the non-suspense versions. Let me use `ClientSideSuspense` which is the recommended approach:

Now I need to update `liveblocks.config.ts` to export the non-suspense hooks (since I'm using `ClientSideSuspense` instead of the suspense versions). Let me check what `ClientSideSuspense` does with the hook variant - the suspense hooks should be used inside `ClientSideSuspense`. Let me re-check the docs pattern.

Looking at the Liveblocks docs: when using `createRoomContext`, you get `suspense.useStorage` etc. for use inside Suspense boundaries, and the non-suspense `useStorage` outside. With `ClientSideSuspense`, the inner component should use the suspense hooks. But the error was that the _page_ was trying to pre-render with the suspense hooks.

The issue is that `ClientSideSuspense` from `@liveblocks/react` itself ensures client-only rendering. The inner component with `useStorage` from `suspense` should work inside it. But the problem was the `Room.tsx` was running the `RoomProvider` from `.suspense` server-side too.

Let me check what the Room component is doing - it uses the `RoomProvider` from `suspense`. The `RoomProvider` should be fine server-side. The issue is specifically with the `useStorage` suspense hook being called during SSR. Let me update the config to use the classic hooks (non-suspense) and wrap with `ClientSideSuspense`:

input.result:
The multiplayer form is fully implemented. Here's a summary of what was built:

## Files Created/Modified

### `liveblocks.config.ts`
Typed Liveblocks configuration using `createRoomContext` with:
- **Presence**: `{ typing: "name" | "email" | "bio" | null, info: { name: string } }`
- **Storage**: `{ form: LiveObject<{ name: string, email: string, bio: string }> }`
- Exports suspense-variant hooks (`useStorage`, `useMutation`, `useOthers`, `useUpdateMyPresence`) and the `RoomProvider`
- Client authenticates via `authEndpoint: "/api/liveblocks-auth"`

### `app/api/liveblocks-auth/route.ts`
Server-side POST endpoint using `@liveblocks/node` `Liveblocks` class with `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY`. Issues JWT tokens granting `FULL_ACCESS` to the requested room. Verified returning HTTP 200 with a valid JWT.

### `app/Room.tsx` (client component)
Wraps children in `RoomProvider` with:
- Room ID derived from `NEXT_PUBLIC_ROOM_ID` env var (set via `next.config.js` from `ZEALT_RUN_ID`)
- Display name read from `?name=` query param, falling back to `"Anonymous"`
- `initialStorage` with `LiveObject({ name: "", email: "", bio: "" })`

### `next.config.js`
Sets `NEXT_PUBLIC_ROOM_ID` = `harbor-typing-form-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}` (falls back to `harbor-typing-form-local`) so each evaluation run gets an isolated room.

### `app/MultiplayerForm.tsx` (client component)
Full multiplayer form using `ClientSideSuspense` (avoids SSR errors):
- **Storage sync**: each keystroke calls `useMutation` → `storage.get("form").set(field, value)`, syncing across all browsers in the room
- **Presence**: `onFocus` sets `typing: fieldId`, `onBlur` sets `typing: null`
- **Per-field typing indicator**: `data-testid="typing-indicator-{name|email|bio}"` rendered only when ≥1 other user is typing there, showing `"Alice is typing in Bio…"`
- **Per-field lock overlay**: `data-testid="lock-overlay-{name|email|bio}"` absolutely-positioned div with `pointerEvents: "none"` (soft lock — input remains focusable/clickable)
- All three inputs have their required `data-testid` attributes: `input-name` (input), `input-email` (input), `input-bio` (textarea)

### `app/layout.tsx`
Wraps all children in the `<Room>` provider component.

### `app/page.tsx`
Renders `<MultiplayerForm />`.

The build succeeds cleanly (`✓ Compiled successfully`) and `npm run start` serves HTTP 200 on port 3000.
